source: Official marking keys + model answers (6 wines)

principles.descriptor_validity: Aroma/flavour descriptors must be accurate and correct for the wine
principles.flexibility: Examiners accept descriptors beyond the WSET Lexicon if accurate
principles.primary_secondary_tertiary_required: For complex wines, marks require evidence from ALL THREE aroma categories
principles.simple_wine_exception: For demonstrably simple wines, 'simple' can substitute tertiary requirement; remaining marks from primary only
principles.flavour_mirrors_aroma: Palate flavour descriptors should mirror nose aroma descriptors
principles.conclusion_must_be_supported: Quality and readiness conclusions must flow from tasting observations

distinction_level_response: Clear intensity statement for nose and palate
Primary descriptors: specific (e.g., 'lemon, peach, melon, pineapple') not generic ('fruity')
Secondary: MLF (butter/cream/cheese) or oak (vanilla/cedar/smoke/toast) identified and attributed
Tertiary: bottle-age (honey/nutty/petroleum/leather/tobacco/forest floor) identified and attributed
All palate scale elements addressed: sweetness, acidity, [tannin for reds], alcohol, body, intensity, finish
Quality level matches wine complexity evidenced (e.g., very good = secondary+tertiary noted)
Readiness conclusion is logical given development stage observed

common_mark_losses: Generic descriptors: 'fruity', 'complex', 'nice' instead of specific terms
Missing aroma category: only primary noted, no secondary or tertiary
Missing palate elements: forgetting alcohol, body, or finish
Quality level inconsistent with observed characteristics
Stating 'excelente' without tertiary complexity or long finish
Stating 'demasiado joven' for a developed wine showing bottle-age characters

appearance: clarity statement only if not clear

simple_wine.example: Ca di Alte Pinot Grigio (basic Veneto)
simple_wine.nose_strategy: Note as 'simple'; list primary aromas; do NOT attempt to invent tertiary

premium_wine.nose_strategy: Must identify primary, secondary (oak/MLF), AND tertiary (bottle age) to earn all marks
premium_wine.palate_strategy: Full structured response across all elements; complexity acknowledged

outstanding_wine.example: Ata Rangi McCrone Pinot Noir, Château Doisy-Daëne Barsac
outstanding_wine.nose_strategy: Rich tertiary development; pronounced intensity; complex layered aromas
outstanding_wine.palate_strategy: High structure; long finish; excellent balance
outstanding_wine.readiness_expected: drink now but has potential for ageing
